page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7se.6xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 24 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core, running on an Intel Xeon 8369B processor clocked at 2.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 48.0 GB of RAM, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps. The underlying Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX, AVX-512 Foundation, AVX-512 Vector Neural Network Instructions (VNNI), and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA-NI.

In benchmark evaluations, the ecs.c7se.6xlarge demonstrates consistent mid-range performance across compute, memory, and specialized workloads. Single-core CPU performance via stress-ng is measured at 1845.48, while multi-core performance reaches 23294.91. Memory bandwidth tests show 1256.5 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, dropping to 129.22 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads and 63.67 GB/sec for large-block writes. The instance achieves a Geekbench multi-core score of 12129.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 29295.6. For database operations, it handles Redis SET operations at 23.8 million operations per second and scores 11220.94 on Passmark database tests. Notably, its text generation speed for a small 135M LLM model registers a strong performance of 293.82 tokens per second, placing it in the top 25% tier, though larger 7B model text generation drops to an average of 15.59 tokens per second.

The primary tradeoff of the ecs.c7se.6xlarge is its compute-heavy, memory-lean profile, offering only 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU and no local storage. This configuration is highly cost-efficient for CPU-bound tasks that do not require massive memory footprints or high-speed local disk access. It is appropriate for workloads such as static web serving (achieving 27.97 GB/sec throughput), software compilation (scoring 21575.0 on Geekbench clang compilation), multi-threaded decompression, and lightweight machine learning inference.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7se.6xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 24 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.9 GHz, paired with 48.0 GB of RAM. Lacking local storage and GPU hardware, the instance relies on network-attached storage and offers an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. It delivers balanced, mid-range benchmark performance, including a Passmark CPU score of 29295.6 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 12129.0. It shows strong performance in small-model LLM text generation at 293.82 tokens per second, alongside steady database capabilities with 23.8 million Redis SET operations per second. With a 2.0 GB per core memory ratio, this instance represents a cost-efficient option for CPU-intensive, memory-lean workloads such as software compilation, static web serving, and lightweight inference tasks.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7se.6xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 24 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 48 GB RAM, and 8 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for CPU-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7se.6xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 24 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 48 GB RAM, with no local storage.
tagline: A dedicated compute-optimized instance featuring twenty-four Intel Xeon cores and forty-eight gigabytes of RAM for processing-heavy workloads.
bullet_points: 24 dedicated Intel Xeon 8369B vCPUs running at 2.9 GHz
48.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Strong text generation performance of 293.82 tokens/sec on small LLM models
Appropriate for software compilation, static web serving, and lightweight inference
